item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reluctant -1. While I share a lot of the same ideas and politics of the artist(s), it would seem, as an SCP, it feels forced, and in some places, too much like author insertion.

Can I make a suggestion? Could this be a tale? Take the Foundation out of it, and have this be a slice-of-life walk-through of an AWCY installation. Hell, you could even keep the perpetual nature of it, ie, someone stumbles on it accidentally. Hell, I'd help you convert this/write it. I like the idea you have here, I just don't think it's a mainlister.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was trying to find a word to describe my opinion. Bittermixin did this for me. This skip is pretty much just a glorified Log of Anomalous Items.

But I would like to add a twist of sorts, and this is the reason I'm staying with no-vote instead of downvote:

This is a glorified Log of Anomalous ART. And I actually like that
Let me tell you why

You see, the problem with most AWCY pieces is that the artist isn't just anomalous, but a sociopath. I mean, 1057 is good, but it kills too much. I'm not on board with the trend of AWCY as a Art Terrorists.

Here we have what could very well be described as a "legit" typical Anart Gallery. The artist isn't a genius, and most art projects in real life aren't strokes of genius either. The messages are old and stale, but they are the kind of stale and old message that hook up most of us until the early twenties. It is probably well within the author intentions if you think about it, because you have it all: the piece against the government, the piece about individuality, the piece to ridicule the guy I don't like, the piece against ignorance, and so on, and so forth. This is very good precisely because it shows that the artist isn't as special as they think to be, but is anomalous nonetheless.


What keeps me from upvoting, though, are two problems. The first being that the Foundation pretty much does nothing with it. Their participation is limited before the article simply turns into the catalogue of the gallery. On several sections we have things happening that should result in some kind of research, or at least a commentary of the research team, like in the case of -4.

And the other is that it does nothing else with the concept. As much as I liked this, there is already SCP-2739, an equally common anart gallery, with the article serving more as a catalogue, but where a story is told. The Foundation does research on the artist, and more important than the message of her work is that the pieces together tell you why she wanted to do it that way. You can disagree with her pieces, think them bland, and still be touched by the backstory of the skip.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really dislike this. I’m going to have to agree with Shaggydredlocks in that it doesn’t actually go anywhere beyond describing a bunch of silly and well-trodden concepts. Without a sufficient development, it’s a glorified Log of Anomalous Items.

SCP-1057 will remain my absolute favourite AWCY piece. It’s snappy, it hooks you immediately, and it plays off its own concept wonderfully.

Panic means that the idea of a shark can be more
dangerous than an actual shark.

It can even be more dangerous than no shark at all.

Are we cool yet?

That stinger line carries so much weight and it’s staying power is, in my opinion, unrivalled. This boils down to the same tired old observations that a thousand comedians and online communities have made a thousand times before, and insults its reader’s intelligence by pretending they’re something more.

You could argue that it’s meant to be vapid and pointless, but that doesn’t make it any less vapid and pointless.

Definite downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
tbh, not to be rude at all, some of this looks like something out of those /r/im14andthisisdeep posts. now, if this is supposed to be like that, then I guess it's pretty clever, and I would upvote.

If this was meant to be a message, then no vote, because you had some good imagery but what I have been seeing has been done tons of times before.

rn, i'm novoting because i'm not sure at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I don't know what I expected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: what are you talking about this is incredibly insightful

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: if people think celebrities are untalented, why are they always constantly giving them attention by making videos making fun of them and calling them untalented?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
my muscles, my muscles

involuntarily flex

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Youtube 2013

I'm not sure what part of what I said you thought sounded like "stale meme that never really took off from four years ago on one of the largest social media sites on the planet," but feel free to try again, my friend.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: two trucks having sex

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I challenge anyone here to make a statement about modern society that is insightful, provocative, and has not already been made by someone else on social media.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I downvoted for the pop culture references; they feel mean-spirited and included only for the sake of inclusion on the main list, rather than to add anything to the article.

I rather like the idea of the unchanging exhibit, and what isn't pop-culture is good. It's just not good enough to forgive that flaw.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreeing that the pop culture references feel a little contrived. The article seems to think it's a lot more clever than it actually is. If that's part of the point it didn't work for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a neat idea for an anart display with lots of different anomalous pieces in it. I mean, I like the general concept of "any change you make is meaningless". -5 and -10 are great.

But overall, too much of it is juvenile and pop-culture. I mean, -1, is this an event people are going to recognize the impact of in a few years? Is Kim Kardashian? I hate that you made me write that question. But yes, overall, a little too random and thrown-together.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In the place of Dr. Dao

I think this should include his first name.

I'm not impressed, it feels like a lot of stuff covered before, and not in a good way. A couple things like Popcorn United and the Kardashian part did have nice imagery, but I wasn't feeling it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This fictional vapid artist may have done their job too well, because for me it elicits about the same emotion as a Tracey Emin or Chris Ofili work - I'm sure the artist thought the work was a grand commentary on modern life, but ultimately they are merely contributing nothing.

Now I'm worked up about the garbage that is YBA, onto the actual SCP: I'm not sure all of them are that good or interesting - the only interesting ones really is 5 and 10, and definitely could be developed further.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps, but it doesn't move past that tired 'AWCY sticking it to the plebs' in any meaningful way, and it seriously overstays its welcome for how long it goes on with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the point is that it's supposed to be the story of a vapid artist commenting on vapid things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
'Kim Kardashian is useless. People believe dumb things. Donald Trump is a big ol meanie. Everyone is special. Society is a maciiiiiine!'

This inspired and intellectual artist must really be blowing some lids off with all this keen new insight. It seems their 'never changes' point applies just as much to themselves.

item.title: Re: recently created
item.content:encoded: ah, clever. Wouldn't that make it a little unrelated to the rest of the pieces?

item.title: Re: recently created
item.content:encoded: it's indescribable, seems like

item.title: Re: recently created
item.content:encoded:
it makes me mad
you rhymed thing, with thing and thing

that aside, upvoting for the implications about our current culture. I can't wrap my head around what's going on with -5 though.

item.title: recently created
item.content:encoded:
This is a thing.
A very thing thing.
Limerick is bad.
